extraction.one_line_summary: PHILIPSEN Jasper won the sprint after VANDERPOEL Mathieu executed a perfect leadout, narrowly avoiding relegation for line deviation in final meters
tactical_patterns: POWERS Neilson orchestrated early KOM breakaway deal with PICHON, trading climbing points for sprint points through rider etiquette.
Alpecin-Deceuninck dominated final kilometer positioning through technical 180-degree bend outside 2km, catching disorganized sprint trains.
SIMMONS Quinn active for Trek early but failed to deliver PEDERSEN to front, showing leadout weakness for hilly flat stages.
CAVENDISH Mark trapped behind KAASBOL 192 position at 2km, revealing Astana's inability to position through technical final turns.

rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: won despite line deviation investigation, shows confidence taking risks in tight finishes for marginal gains
rider_signals.VANDERPOEL Mathieu: positive: executed textbook 500m leadout from technical bend to flamme rouge, rare domestique perfection from classics star
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: negative: absent from final despite SIMMONS early work, Trek's sprint train lacks cohesion on semi-technical finishes
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: caught outside top-20 positions at 2km behind weak KAASBOL pilot, age showing in technical positioning battles
rider_signals.POWERS Neilson: positive: proactive KOM defense through negotiated breakaway within 14km, smart jersey management without wasting team resources
